column5: Inline setTimeout/clearTimeout solution (~11 lines) applied directly to the single search input, no factory abstraction, no unrequested extras.

The answer correctly implements a database UNIQUE constraint on (owner_id, name) as the enforcement mechanism and limits app-side code to handling the 23505 error gracefully.
Enforces uniqueness via database UNIQUE constraint on (owner_id, name), with app-side error handling limited to displaying the 23505 violation—no app-side checks or unnecessary procedures.
Database UNIQUE constraint on (owner_id, name) is the enforcement mechanism; app-side code is limited to error handling without app-side checks or race conditions.
Working implementation and comprehensive runnable Jest tests covering happy-path combinations (1h30m, 1h30m45s), single units (45s, 2m, 1h), and multiple invalid-input error paths.
Working implementation present with comprehensive runnable Jest tests covering happy-path combinations (1h30m, 1h30m45s), single units (1s, 30m, 2h), and 11 error cases that properly throw.
Parameterized queries throughout, valid 404 on invalid/already-redeemed coupons, and WHERE used_at IS NULL atomically gates double-redemption.

Coupon code used in parameterized query with explicit invalid-coupon error path (400); UPDATE with WHERE code=$1 AND redeemed_at IS NULL provides atomicity against double-redemption; transaction wraps both updates.
Parameterized queries throughout, explicit 404 on missing/redeemed coupon, and atomic UPDATE with redeemed_by IS NULL condition prevents double-crediting.
